mnemonics.title: ER Diagram Components
mnemonics.content: Remember ER Diagram has 'EAR': Entity (Rectangle), Attribute (Oval), Relationship (Diamond)

mnemonics.title: Key Types Order
mnemonics.content: Remember keys hierarchy: Super > Candidate > Primary > Foreign. Think: 'S Catch Pretty Fast' - Super Catch Primary Foreign

mnemonics.title: Data Model Types
mnemonics.content: Remember main models with 'HNRO': Hierarchical, Network, Relational, Object-oriented

mnemonics.title: Cardinality Types
mnemonics.content: Remember 1:1, 1:N, N:M as One-to-One, One-to-Many, Many-to-Many

acronyms.usage: ER Model, ER Diagram - describes entities and their relationships
Software system to manage databases like MySQL, Oracle
Commands to define database schema like CREATE, ALTER
Commands to manipulate data like INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE
Unique identifier for each record in a table
Key that references primary key of another table
Represents absence of a value in database fields

visualTips.visualization: Think of weak entity like a child - cannot exist without parent. Represented by double rectangle, always has partial key combined with owner's primary key
Total participation = MUST exist (shown by double line). Partial = CAN exist without relationship (shown by single line). Double line = mandatory, single line = optional
Crow's foot (three-pronged) = 'Many' side. Single line = 'One' side. Remember: More lines = More options for related entities
Composite = one oval inside another (hierarchical breakdown). Multivalued = double oval (multiple values possible)

analogies.analogy: Entity is like a single person (John), Entity Set is like all people in a city - the collection
Like Aadhaar/ID number - uniquely identifies one person among billions
Like verb connecting two nouns - 'Student studies Course', 'Employee works in Department'
Schema is like blueprint of a building (plan), Instance is the actual built building with people living in it
